title: SysExtension plug-in pattern: attribute-driven factory instead of a switch
instruction: In the sandbox model, replace a construct-switch with the SysExtension plug-in pattern. Ground it first with get_knowledge(topic="sysextension"). Create: (1) a base enum DemoChannelType with elements Web, Store and Partner (labelled, extensible); (2) a class DemoChannelAttribute extending SysAttribute with a parm method carrying the DemoChannelType value, so it can decorate an implementation class; (3) an abstract class DemoChannelHandler with `public abstract str describe()` and a `public static DemoChannelHandler construct(DemoChannelType _channel)` that resolves the implementation through the SysExtension framework (SysExtensionAppClassFactory with the attribute instance as the key) - a switch statement over the enum in construct() FAILS this case; (4) two concrete handlers DemoChannelHandlerWeb and DemoChannelHandlerStore, each decorated with the attribute for its enum value and each returning a labelled string from describe(). Confirm the SysExtension factory member signature with search/get_method before writing it - do not guess. All objects must build with zero BP errors.
